title: PlainCloak v1 keystore (informative)
description: Informative companion schema for an on-disk JSON keystore that PlainCloak implementations MAY use to persist private keys (encrypted at rest) and trusted contact public keys. This file is NOT part of the wire protocol; v1 conformance does not require its use. An implementation that elects to support this schema MUST do so completely (closed-object, all required fields present).

kdf.description: Key derivation function used to derive the AEAD key that encrypts each private_key_enc. RECOMMENDED: Argon2id. PBKDF2 with high iteration counts is acceptable but not preferred.

params.description: Algorithm-specific parameters. For argon2id: m_kib, t (iterations), p (parallelism). For pbkdf2-sha256: iterations.

aead.description: Authenticated-encryption algorithm used to wrap each private_key_enc.

label.description: Human-readable label assigned by the user (e.g. 'Personal', 'Work').

public_key_pem.description: SPKI-PEM serialization of the public key, see spec/v1/08-crypto-suites.md section 8.3.1.

salt.description: Base64 of the KDF salt; SHOULD be 16 random bytes.

nonce.description: Base64 of the AEAD nonce; SHOULD be 12 random bytes for AES-GCM.

ciphertext.description: Base64 of the AEAD ciphertext over the PKCS#8 PEM private key bytes.

expires_at.description: Optional key-rotation deadline (NOT a message-expiry field). Implementations SHOULD warn when a key past this deadline is used to produce or consume messages.

verified_at.description: Time at which the user last confirmed this public key out-of-band. Optional. Implementations MAY warn if verified_at is older than a threshold.
